Name: Indur Vinay
City: Hyderabad
Branch: participant/indurvinay-Hyderabad


UC-0A — Complaint Classifier

Which failure mode did you encounter?
(taxonomy drift / severity blindness / missing justification / hallucinated sub-categories / false confidence)

Severity blindness and taxonomy drift.

Provide one example where the naive prompt failed:

Complaint: “Child fell into pothole near school entrance.”
The naive classifier marked it as Standard priority and used a category like Road Issue, which is not part of the allowed taxonomy.

After your fix — are all category labels restricted to the allowed list?

Yes — all categories are restricted to the allowed schema values defined in the README.

Does your classifier flag genuinely ambiguous complaints?

Yes — ambiguous complaints are flagged using NEEDS_REVIEW.

Your git commit message for UC-0A:

UC-0A Fix severity blindness: urgent keywords ignored → added injury/child/school detection and strict category enforcement

UC-0B — Summary That Changes Meaning

Which failure mode did you encounter?
(clause omission / scope bleed / obligation softening)

Clause omission and obligation softening.

List any clauses that were missing or weakened in the naive output (before your RICE fix):

Clause 2.6 (carry-forward limit), Clause 5.2 (dual approval requirement), and Clause 7.2 (leave encashment prohibition) were missing or softened.

After your fix — are all 10 critical clauses present in summary_hr_leave.txt?

Yes — all 10 clauses are preserved in the summary with their original obligations intact.

Did the naive prompt add any information not in the source document (scope bleed)?

Yes — the naive summary added phrases like “as is standard HR practice,” which were not present in the source document.

Your git commit message for UC-0B:

UC-0B Fix clause omission: naive summary dropped conditions → enforced clause inventory and full clause preservation

UC-0C — Number That Looks Right

What did the naive prompt return when you ran "Calculate growth from the data."?

The naive output returned a single aggregated growth value across all wards without specifying the ward or category.

Did it aggregate across all wards? Did it mention the 5 null rows?

Yes — it aggregated across wards and did not mention the 5 null rows in the dataset.

After your fix — does your system refuse all-ward aggregation?

Yes.

Does your growth_output.csv flag the 5 null rows rather than skipping them?

Yes — flagged rows include:

2024-03 · Ward 2 – Shivajinagar · Drainage & Flooding

2024-07 · Ward 4 – Warje · Roads & Pothole Repair

2024-11 · Ward 1 – Kasba · Waste Management

2024-08 · Ward 3 – Kothrud · Parks & Greening

2024-05 · Ward 5 – Hadapsar · Streetlight Maintenance

Does your output match the reference values (Ward 1 Roads +33.1% in July, −34.8% in October)?

Yes — the MoM growth values match the reference values in the README.

Your git commit message for UC-0C:

UC-0C Fix silent null handling: null rows ignored → added null detection and ward-category scoped growth computation

UC-X — Ask My Documents

What did the naive prompt return for the cross-document test question?
(Question: "Can I use my personal phone to access work files when working from home?")

The naive answer blended HR and IT policy and stated that personal devices could access work tools when working remotely.

Did it blend the IT and HR policies?

Yes — it incorrectly combined information from HR remote-work guidance and IT device policies.

After your fix — what does your system return for this question?

“According to policy_it_acceptable_use.txt section 3.1, personal devices may only access CMC email and the employee self-service portal.”

Did your system use any hedging phrases in any answer?

No — hedging phrases such as “typically,” “generally,” or “while not explicitly covered” are blocked by the enforcement rules.

Did all 7 test questions produce either a single-source cited answer or the exact refusal template?

Yes — all questions returned either a single-source answer with document citation or the refusal template.

Your git commit message for UC-X:

UC-X Fix cross-document blending: naive QA merged HR and IT policies → enforced single-source retrieval with refusal template

CRAFT Loop Reflection

Which CRAFT step was hardest across all UCs, and why?

The hardest step was Testing and Refinement. Several naive prompts produced plausible but incorrect outputs, especially with clause omission and cross-document blending. Identifying those subtle failures required repeated testing and stricter enforcement rules.

What is the single most important thing you added manually to an agents.md that the AI did not generate on its own?

A strict rule preventing cross-document blending:
“Never combine claims from two different documents into a single answer.”

Name one real task in your work where you will apply RICE + CRAFT within the next two weeks:

I will apply RICE + CRAFT when building an internal document QA assistant for project documentation, ensuring answers are sourced from a single document and include citations.
